whenToUse: Multiple interchangeable algorithms for the same task
Behavior needs to be swappable at runtime
Giant if-else/switch deciding which behavior to execute
You want to add new behaviors without modifying existing code
whenNotToUse: Only one algorithm that won't change (YAGNI)
The behavior change is state-driven, not client-driven (use State)
The algorithms share 80%+ of their code (use Template Method)
You need to notify others about changes (use Observer)

structure.Context: Holds Strategy interface reference, delegates calls
structure.ConcreteStrategy: Each algorithm in its own class
structure.Client: Creates and injects the chosen strategy into Context

cheatSheet.sixWordSummary: The Context stays. The Strategy swaps.
cheatSheet.codeSmellTrigger: if-else / switch on type to decide behavior

bestPractices: Keep the Strategy interface focused -- one method for one algorithm
Use a Factory to create strategies when selection logic is complex
Always provide a Null Object default strategy to avoid null crashes
Never use instanceof or type-checking in the Context
Apply YAGNI -- only use Strategy when behavior actually varies
Each concrete strategy should follow SRP -- do one thing well
Prefer composition (Strategy) over inheritance for interchangeable algorithms
The Context should be blissfully ignorant of which concrete strategy it holds

commonMistakes.mistake: Using instanceof in the Context to check which strategy is set
commonMistakes.correct: Move logic into the strategy itself -- the Context just delegates

commonMistakes.mistake: Putting validation, logging, and notifications inside the strategy
commonMistakes.correct: Strategy does ONE thing. Use Decorator for cross-cutting concerns

commonMistakes.mistake: Creating Strategy when there's only one algorithm that won't change
commonMistakes.correct: Ask YAGNI: will this actually vary? If not, just call the method directly

commonMistakes.mistake: Forgetting to set a strategy before calling the Context method
commonMistakes.correct: Initialize with a Null Object default: private Strategy s = new NoOpStrategy()
